userStories.title: Wire plugin loading into the runner startup
userStories.description: The runner (src/execution/runner.ts) should call loadPlugins() at startup and store the resulting PluginRegistry in the pipeline context. Currently loadPlugins() exists in src/plugins/loader.ts but is never called. The runner should: 1) resolve global dir (~/.nax/plugins), project dir (<workdir>/nax/plugins), and config.plugins[] entries, 2) call loadPlugins(), 3) store the registry on the RunContext or pass it through the pipeline. On teardown (run end), call registry.teardownAll().
acceptanceCriteria: Runner calls loadPlugins() during initialization before story loop starts
PluginRegistry is accessible from pipeline context (RunContext or similar)
registry.teardownAll() is called on both success and failure paths
If no plugins are found, an empty registry is used (no error)
Plugin loading errors are logged but do not abort the run

userStories.title: Context providers inject into agent prompts
userStories.description: Before agent execution in the pipeline, call all registered context providers (registry.getContextProviders()) for the current story. Each provider returns {content, estimatedTokens, label}. Append provider content to the agent prompt under labeled sections, respecting a configurable token budget (default 2000 tokens total for all providers). If a provider throws, log warning and skip it. Add contextProviderTokenBudget to ExecutionConfig.
acceptanceCriteria: All registered context providers are called before agent execution
Provider content is appended to the prompt under a markdown section with the provider's label
Total injected tokens respect the token budget (providers are added in order until budget exhausted)
Provider errors are caught, logged, and skipped without failing the story
Token budget is configurable via execution.contextProviderTokenBudget in config

userStories.title: Review plugins run after built-in verification
userStories.description: After the built-in review checks (typecheck/lint/test) pass in the pipeline's review stage (src/pipeline/stages/), call all registered reviewer plugins (registry.getReviewers()). Each reviewer's check(workdir, changedFiles) returns {passed, output, exitCode?}. If any reviewer fails, treat it the same as a built-in check failure (triggers retry/escalation). Include reviewer output in the story result for debugging.
acceptanceCriteria: Plugin reviewers run after built-in checks pass
Each reviewer receives the workdir and list of changed files
Reviewer failure triggers the same retry/escalation as built-in check failures
Reviewer output is included in the story execution result
If a reviewer throws an exception, it counts as a failure with the exception message as output
Multiple reviewers run sequentially; first failure short-circuits remaining reviewers

userStories.title: Reporter plugins receive lifecycle events
userStories.description: Fire reporter events at the appropriate points in the runner loop: onRunStart when the run begins, onStoryComplete after each story finishes (success or failure), onRunEnd when the run completes. All reporter calls are fire-and-forget (errors logged, never block pipeline). Build event objects from existing run state (RunStartEvent, StoryCompleteEvent, RunEndEvent as defined in src/plugins/types.ts).
acceptanceCriteria: onRunStart fires once at run start with runId, feature, totalStories, startTime
onStoryComplete fires after each story with storyId, status, durationMs, cost, tier, testStrategy
onRunEnd fires once at run end with runId, totalDurationMs, totalCost, storySummary counts
Reporter errors are caught and logged but never block execution
Multiple reporters all receive events (not short-circuited on error)
Events fire even when the run fails or is aborted (onRunEnd still fires)

userStories.title: Plugin routing strategies integrate into router chain
userStories.description: Insert plugin routing strategies (registry.getRouters()) before the built-in router in the routing chain (src/routing/). When routing a story, iterate plugin routers first; if any returns a routing decision, use it. If all plugin routers return null/undefined, fall back to the built-in strategy. This allows plugins to override routing for specific stories (e.g., always route security-tagged stories to premium).
acceptanceCriteria: Plugin routers are tried before the built-in routing strategy
First plugin router that returns a non-null result wins
If all plugin routers return null, built-in strategy is used as fallback
Plugin routers receive the same story context as built-in routers
Router errors are caught and logged; fallback to next router in chain

userStories.title: CLI 'nax plugins list' command
userStories.description: Add a 'plugins' subcommand to the CLI (src/cli/) with a 'list' action. It should: 1) load plugins from global + project + config sources (reuse loadPlugins), 2) display a table with name, version, provides[], and source (global/project/config), 3) exit cleanly. If no plugins found, print a helpful message pointing to docs.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'nax plugins list' prints a formatted table of installed plugins
Each row shows: plugin name, version, extension types provided, source location
If no plugins found, prints 'No plugins installed' with help text
Works in both project directory and outside (global-only scan outside project)
Exit code 0 on success

userStories.title: Read plugin config entries from nax config.json
userStories.description: The config loader (src/config/loader.ts) already supports a plugins[] array in the schema. Ensure that when config is loaded, the plugins[] entries are passed to loadPlugins() as the configPlugins parameter. Each entry has {module, config?}. Validate that module paths resolve correctly (relative to project root). Add a helpful error message if a configured plugin module cannot be found.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins[] from config.json are passed to loadPlugins() as configPlugins parameter
Relative module paths in plugins[].module are resolved relative to project root
Absolute module paths and npm package names work as-is
If a plugin module cannot be found, a clear error message is logged with the path tried
Plugin-specific config (plugins[].config) is passed to the plugin's setup() function

userStories.title: Sample console reporter plugin with tests
userStories.description: Create a sample reporter plugin at examples/plugins/console-reporter/ that demonstrates the plugin API. It should: 1) implement IReporter with onRunStart (print banner), onStoryComplete (print status line), onRunEnd (print summary table), 2) have a setup() that reads config.verbose for detail level, 3) include unit tests in the same directory. This serves as both documentation and a smoke test for the plugin system.
acceptanceCriteria: Plugin exports a valid NaxPlugin with provides: ['reporter']
onRunStart prints a run banner with feature name and story count
onStoryComplete prints a status line (✓/✗) with story ID, tier, duration, cost
onRunEnd prints a summary table with total stories, pass/fail counts, total cost, total duration
setup() reads config.verbose (boolean, default false) for extra detail
Plugin passes validatePlugin() from src/plugins/validator.ts
Unit tests verify all three event handlers produce expected output
